content:
First write \(r=[r]+\{r\}=n+f\) with \(n\in\mathbb{Z}_{\ge0}\), \(0\le f<1\).  The equation  
\[
25f+n=125
\]
gives 
\[
f=\frac{125-n}{25}.
\]
We require \(0\le f<1\), i.e.
\[
0\le\frac{125-n}{25}<1
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
n\le125,\quad n>100
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
n=101,102,\dots,125.
\]
That is 25 values of \(n\).  For each,
\[
r_n=n+\frac{125-n}{25}.
\]
Hence
\[
\sum r_n
=\sum_{n=101}^{125}n\;+\;\sum_{n=101}^{125}\frac{125-n}{25}
=2825+12=2837.
\]
Answer: \(\boxed{2837}\).
